cold_open.hook: Cisco just posted record quarterly revenue while firing 3,900 people—the most profitable layoff in corporate theater history.

markets.macro_note: Broad rally on AI-spending narrative; mega-cap tech softening while infrastructure plays surge.

big_story.story_title: Russian government hackers exposed trying to hijack Signal accounts
big_story.angle: A spyware investigator just caught Russia's FSB red-handed attempting to compromise Signal—the encrypted app everyone trusts. This is the moment the security theater meets actual theater: the tool designed to defeat state surveillance is now the prize state actors are actively hunting.

quick_hits.angle: Dell's Alienware brand is getting watered down by a RAM shortage rebrand—this is what happens when you let supply chain dictate your identity.
UK economy grew faster than expected—the one place where inflation theater actually produced a plot twist.
Kevin Warsh confirmed as Fed leader while Trump tries to make deals in China—two parallel universes of economic policy colliding in real time.
Honda posts its first annual loss in 70 years—a company that survived the 1970s oil crisis just got taken down by the EV transition it saw coming.

odd_thing.joke_angle: You can make an app for that—the most millennial headline ever written, now true for literally everything including your own obsolescence.

sign_off.callback_target: Cisco's 'record revenue while firing 3,900 people' — the most profitable layoff in corporate theater history
